response:
I began with round 0, checking the first values that can be verified independently.

1. Convert the binary h and S1 reported on the trace to hex:  
   • h (L0069) = 0101 1011 1110 0000 1100 1101 0001 1001 → 0x5BE0CD19  
   • S1 (L0070) = 0011 0101 1000 0111 0010 0111 0010 1011 → 0x3587272B  

2. Add them modulo 2³²:  
   0x5BE0CD19 + 0x3587272B = 0x9147F444

3. Compare with the trace’s “step1” (the same addition) at L0072:  
   L0072 shows step1 = 1001 0001 0110 0111 1111 0100 0100 0100 → 0x9167F444

   The trace value differs from the correct result by 0x00200000 (one single-bit difference).

Since step1 is already wrong, every value that follows in the trace is derived from this incorrect word, so all later operations can still be self-consistent. The very first round already disagrees with the real SHA-256 computation, proving the transcript has been tampered with.

VERDICT: TAMPERED  
ROUND: 0  
REASONING: step1 at L0072 should be 0x9147F444 (h + S1), but the trace gives 0x9167F444, differing by one bit; thus the error starts in round 0 and propagates through the rest of the trace.
